SprintScope: Boundary-reduction sprint: manuscript cleanup, quotient descent, source representability, witness-comparison reduction, and branchwise Jcan regularity.

Ledger.Assumptions: Finite-dimensional surrogate for the seven Main-facing interface clauses.
Ledger.Method: Exact association validation plus 3 valid and 3 invalid synthetic examples.
SurrogateBlindSpots: Closedness is automatic/invisible at finite dimension.
Continuity of chi is automatic for finite-dimensional linear maps.
Hilbertizability is reduced to positive-definite matrix data, not the abstract completion statement.
Ledger.RepresentativeModelFamily: valid_basic_identity, valid_nontrivial_gram, valid_zero_ambiguity; invalid_parity_tag, invalid_chi_on_ambiguity, invalid_positive_form
Ledger.Acceptance: 3 valid accepted; 3 invalid rejected for the expected reason.

Ledger.Assumptions: Branchwise rho^dom, nu, Aminus, and Jdesc family on an accepted interval.
Ledger.Method: Resolve/Reduce on an exact polynomial branch model, connected-component audit, and broken-model hypothesis drops.
SurrogateBlindSpots: Continuity is certified only for the chosen exact model family.
The theorem is not universally proved for all admissible branches.

Ledger.Assumptions: Transport theorem discharged; chi = nu^-1 rho^dom defines the weak Main export.
Ledger.Method: Exact export chi = nu^-1 rho^dom with affine constancy, sign-defined components, and ambiguity-leak counterexample.
SurrogateBlindSpots: The weak export is checked on exact branch models rather than universally.
Ledger.RepresentativeModelFamily: certified_polynomial_branch with explicit broken hypothesis models

Ledger.Assumptions: chi, Jsharp, beta > 0, and a coercive form on ker chi.
Ledger.Method: Exact basis-aware ambient construction B = M^-T G M^-1 with coercive kernel metric.
SurrogateBlindSpots: Coercivity and Hilbertizability are represented by positive-definite finite matrices.
No infinite-dimensional completion is constructed.
Ledger.RepresentativeModelFamily: Exact basis-aware ambient calibration examples with broken-input countermodels

Ledger.Assumptions: Weak 2T_char interface and a positive symmetric B on the affine class.
Ledger.Method: Exact affine projection with GA = U^T B U and Jcan = J0 + U a*.
SurrogateBlindSpots: Closed affine subspace and Hilbert projection are realized by finite-dimensional matrix algebra.
Ledger.RepresentativeModelFamily: Exact full-column-rank affine examples with rank/B/chi countermodels

Ledger.Assumptions: Finite-dimensional affine class and positive B.
Ledger.Method: Exact orthogonality, projector formula, and minimality-difference identity.
SurrogateBlindSpots: The proof is finite-dimensional and does not replace the abstract Hilbert-space statement.
Ledger.RepresentativeModelFamily: Exact projector-model family with representative-shift checks

Ledger.Assumptions: Dominant-response functional and ambiguity subspace on the finite-cutoff odd layer.
Ledger.Method: Exact quotient coordinates W, descended covector eta, and universal descent equivalences via Resolve.
SurrogateBlindSpots: Topological quotient continuity is represented by exact finite-dimensional linear maps.
Closure of the ambiguity span is not separately audited beyond the surrogate.
Ledger.RepresentativeModelFamily: Exact quotient-descent valid/broken model pair with nu != 1 obstruction witness

Ledger.Assumptions: Descended dominant functional on the ambiguity quotient and the affine descendant class.
Ledger.Method: Exact affine-class constancy and quotient-value reduction on J0 + Aminus.
SurrogateBlindSpots: The quotient point [![Xi^(1)]!] is represented by exact coordinates rather than an abstract completion object.
Ledger.RepresentativeModelFamily: Exact affine descendant class with quotient-point scalar reduction

Ledger.Assumptions: Fixed-cutoff image-membership problem Pi_phys R(F_tau) = Pi_phys T[tau].
Ledger.Method: Exact image-membership solver for Pi_phys R(F_tau) = Pi_phys T[tau], with obstruction and gauge-space extraction.
SurrogateBlindSpots: Sufficient locality remains imported and is not identified with algebraic image membership.
Only fixed-cutoff linear realizability is audited here.
Ledger.RepresentativeModelFamily: Exact representable unique/family examples plus an explicit obstruction example

Ledger.Assumptions: Representative search on J0 + Aminus versus scalar compatibility of dominant pairings.
Ledger.Method: Exact representative-search reduction to rho-level scalar compatibility, plus obstruction witness for the unscaled chi-level claim when nu != 1.
SurrogateBlindSpots: The notebook certifies the scalar reduction at fixed cutoff only.
The unscaled chi-compatibility claim fails unless an extra normalization hypothesis is added.

Ledger.Assumptions: Rational or polynomial branch data U(lambda), B(lambda), J0(lambda) with invertible Gram block.
Ledger.Method: Exact projector formula with rational-function continuity certified away from Gram-block degeneracy.
SurrogateBlindSpots: Only rational/polynomial finite-cutoff branch data are audited.
The statement does not address infinite-dimensional branch regularity.
Ledger.RepresentativeModelFamily: Exact valid and Gram-degenerate branch examples
Ledger.Source: Main/appendix_main.tex:1837-1870 plus finite-dimensional projector formula remark

Ledger.Assumptions: Certified compressed spectral regime and rank-one LoG operator.
SurrogateBlindSpots: Branchwise smooth transport of the projector may remain an imported input.

Ledger.Assumptions: Canonical representative plus imported bracket axioms on a concrete model.
SurrogateBlindSpots: Bracket locality and AQFT/HLS semantics stay imported.
Only explicit concrete models can be machine-audited here.
